meta.name: Quick Stovetop Flatbread (Pan)
meta.subtitle: Fast • flexible • works across many cuisines • fresh milled or commercial flour

source.notes: A fast, skillet-cooked flatbread with a simple dough. Includes yeast-free and yeast options, plus fresh-milled guidance and multi-cuisine seasoning paths.

yield.notes: Makes 6 medium flatbreads (~7–8 inches). Scale up easily.

tasteProfile.notes: Neutral base flatbread; can go buttery, garlicky, herbed, or spiced depending on cuisine.

ingredients.label: Flour (all-purpose / bread flour / fresh milled wheat flour—see Fresh-Milled Notes)
ingredients.notes: All-purpose is most flexible. Bread flour yields slightly chewier flatbreads.

ingredients.label: Sugar (optional, helps browning)
ingredients.notes: About 1 1/2 teaspoons. Omit for very savory flatbreads.

ingredients.label: Neutral oil or olive oil (in dough)
Plain yogurt (or sour cream) (optional, softens crumb)
ingredients.notes: If omitted, replace with equal weight of water.

ingredients.label: Warm water (or room temp if using yogurt)
ingredients.notes: Hold back 20–30g and add as needed. If omitting yogurt, total water becomes 270g.

ingredients.label: Instant yeast (optional for extra puff + flavor)
ingredients.notes: About 1 teaspoon. If used, allow a longer rest (30–60 minutes) for best flavor.

ingredients.label: Oil or butter for cooking (as needed)
ingredients.notes: Use a thin film; add more between breads if pan dries.

ingredients.label: Butter or garlic butter to brush (optional)

steps.title: Mix dry ingredients
instructions: In a bowl, whisk flour, salt, baking powder, and sugar (if using).
If using optional yeast, whisk it in now.

steps.title: Add wet ingredients and form dough
instructions: Add oil, yogurt (if using), and most of the water (hold back ~20–30g).
Stir until shaggy, then knead briefly (1–2 minutes) in the bowl or on the counter just until smooth.
Add remaining water only if dough is dry and cracking. Dough should be soft and slightly tacky, not sticky.

steps.title: Rest dough
instructions: Cover dough and rest 10–15 minutes (quick version) to relax gluten for easier rolling.
If you used yeast, rest 30–60 minutes for better puff and flavor (still works at 15 minutes in a pinch).

steps.title: Divide and shape
instructions: Divide dough into 6 equal pieces (~110g each).
Round into balls; cover pieces not being rolled.
On a lightly floured surface, roll each ball into a thin round (about 1/8 inch / 3 mm).
For softer, thicker flatbreads, roll slightly thicker (3/16 inch / 4–5 mm).

steps.title: Cook in a hot skillet
instructions: Heat a skillet/griddle over medium-high until hot. Lightly oil the surface.
Cook one flatbread 60–90 seconds until bubbles form and underside has brown spots.
Flip and cook 45–75 seconds more. Adjust heat so it browns without burning.
Optional: cover the skillet briefly after flipping to trap steam and encourage puffing.

steps.title: Keep warm + finish
instructions: Stack cooked flatbreads in a towel to keep soft and warm.
Optional: brush with butter or garlic butter while hot.
Serve as wraps, dippers, or alongside stews, curries, kebabs, or hummus.

freshMilledNotes.summary: Fresh milled flour absorbs more water and can be less extensible. A short rest (or blend) improves roll-out and tenderness.
guidelines: Best everyday texture: use 70% fresh milled flour + 30% all-purpose flour for softness and easy rolling.
If using 100% fresh milled (unsifted), expect a slightly denser bread; increase rest time to 20–30 minutes and add water as needed.
Fresh milled flour often needs +10–30g extra water depending on grind and age.
If dough keeps shrinking when rolling, rest another 10 minutes and roll again.

variations.name: Simple water-only flatbread (no yogurt)
variations.change: Omit yogurt and increase water to 270g total.
variations.notes: Still soft; yogurt version is slightly more tender.

variations.name: Naan-style (softer, slightly thicker)
variations.change: Roll slightly thicker (4–5 mm). Cook on medium heat a bit longer; brush with butter after cooking.

variations.name: Tortilla-style (more pliable, less puffy)
variations.change: Omit baking powder; increase oil to 36g; roll thinner. Cook over medium heat for even browning.

variations.name: Middle Eastern za’atar flatbread
variations.change: After rolling, brush lightly with oil and sprinkle za’atar + sesame; cook as directed.

variations.name: Garlic herb flatbread
variations.change: Add 2–3g garlic powder + 2g dried oregano (or mixed herbs) to dry ingredients; brush with garlic butter after cooking.
variations.notes: Pairs with Italian and Mediterranean meals.

variations.name: Spiced flatbread (West African / Caribbean vibe)
variations.change: Add 2g ground ginger + 1g allspice or grains of paradise (if available) + 1–2g chili powder; finish with a little butter and salt.
variations.notes: Great alongside stews and grilled meats.

storage.counter: Best same day. Keep wrapped to prevent drying. Up to 1 day airtight at room temp.
storage.fridge: Up to 4 days airtight. Rewarm briefly in a dry skillet to restore softness.
storage.freezer: Freeze stacked with parchment between pieces up to 2 months. Rewarm from frozen in a skillet or microwave briefly.

notes: Hot pan + quick cook = best texture. If cooking too slowly, breads dry out; if too hot, they burn before cooking through.
Keep dough covered between rolls to prevent skinning and cracking.
For puffier breads: roll slightly thicker and cover the skillet briefly after flipping.

audit.testNotes: Quick stovetop flatbread designed to be cuisine-agnostic and adaptable. Supports yogurt/no-yogurt and yeast/no-yeast paths.
